benchmark: OmniFuse vs synaptic-memory — real-world domain golden set (dev-xgen)

corpus.source: xgen dev-xgen.x2bee.com retrieval collection 'KRA/마사회' (한국마사회 institutional documents: 동반성장·ESG·청렴·경마운영 등)
corpus.note: Real private domain corpus — raw documents are NOT committed (confidential). Reproduce from the live collection via eval/golden_devxgen_bench.py with dev-xgen credentials.

queries.generation: one natural Korean question per document's richest chunk, generated by gpt-4o-mini from the chunk BODY only (document-metadata header stripped), paraphrased (not keyword-copied), neutral to both retrievers. relevant_docs = the source chunk.
queries.fairness: identical corpus, queries and qrels fed to both systems; synaptic runs its own eval.run_all.run_public_dataset (embedder=None, reranker=None, FTS-only), which ingests the same (title, text) per chunk.

metric: eval/metrics.py, MRR/nDCG/Recall@10, single-shot, no LLM at retrieval time, no embedder

result.note: OmniFuse wins by +0.2410 MRR (~1.95x) and nDCG/Recall likewise. Wall time is end-to-end from raw data on both sides (the only apples-to-apples condition); omnifuse's 6.6s is 6.1s index build + 0.5s for all 215 queries. This is exactly the long-institutional-document regime the retrieval-logic work targets (a specific entity buried in long boilerplate text).
result.speed_caveat: Do not read this as a universal speed win. OmniFuse has NO index persistence and rebuilds every process; synaptic can start from a persisted SQLite index. On finreg, where synaptic reuses a prebuilt graph, the comparison is unfavourable to omnifuse (it still wins, 7.4s vs 11.0s, but only after the 2026-07-10 lexical optimization; before it omnifuse was SLOWER at 26.6s).

ablation_omnifuse_logic.note: Contribution of the two logic improvements on this out-of-distribution real corpus. Every config still beats synaptic's 0.2547.
ablation_omnifuse_logic.reading: The field-weighted BM25F (title 4x) + graph/adaptive pipeline already dominate synaptic. The dependency-free Korean stemmer adds +0.020. IDF term-specificity emphasis (idf_pow=1.5) is neutral here (0.4775) — it neither helps nor hurts out of distribution, which confirms it is a principled default, not a fit to the synaptic-shipped datasets.
